person.name: Jane Rannelegh
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Brief Life History of Catherine
When Catherine Stebbing was born about 1484, in Devon, England, her father, John William Stebbing, was 25 and her mother, Alice Elizabeth Borroridge, was 23. She had at least 12 sons and 7 daughters with John Adams of Feene. She died about 1512, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 30.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Rannelegh於1454年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾貝福斯東時，她的父親William Stebbing-6歲，母親Alice5歲。她和Thomas Adams至少有10個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於大約1512年在英國英格蘭薩默塞特享年59歲。

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Rannelegh was born in 1454, in Beverston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Stebbing, was -6 and her mother, Alice, was 5. She had at least 10 sons and 3 daughters with Thomas Adams. She died about 1512, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Rannelegh est née en 1454, à Beverston, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Stebbing, avait -6 ans et sa mère, Alice, avait 5 ans. Elle a eu au moins 10 fils et 3 filles avec Thomas Adams. Elle est décédée environ 1512, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Rannelegh 1454 in Beverston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Stebbing -6 und ihre Mutter Alice 5. Sie hatte mit Thomas Adams mindestens 10 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 1512 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Ranneleghが1454年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアベバーストンで生まれた時，父William Stebbingは-6歳，母Aliceは5歳であった。彼女はThomas Adamsとの間に少なくとも10人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1512年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトで59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Rannelegh(이)가 1454년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 비버스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Stebbing(은)는 -6세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice(은)는 5세였다. 그녀는 Thomas Adams(와)과 적어도 열 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1512년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.
